description:
Implement two Django management commands that keep the DB and git-committed CSV files in sync — no live auto-sync, two explicit directional pushes.

**export_data** (DB → CSV):
- Writes data/parties.csv, data/invoices.csv, data/payments.csv, data/challans.csv, data/opening_balances.csv
- Each CSV has erp_id (DB PK) as first column so future imports can match precisely
- Column names match DEFAULT_MAPPINGS in migration_app/services.py

**import_data** (CSV → DB):
- Classifies each row: NEW / UNCHANGED / MODIFIED / CONFLICT / MISSING
- Matching: by erp_id first, then natural keys (party+invoice_number, party+reference_number, party+challan_number, party+effective_date+balance_type+amount)
- Dry-run mode shows full report without writing
- --accept-csv / --accept-db flags for batch conflict resolution
- --delete-missing to remove DB records absent from CSV (PROTECT FK constraint respected)
- Commits atomically inside transaction.atomic()

**Makefile targets:** export-data, import-data-dry, import-data

**Reuses from migration_app/services.py:** normalize_date(), normalize_decimal(), normalize_boolean()

**New files:** migration_app/management/commands/export_data.py, migration_app/management/commands/import_data.py, data/*.csv, data/README.md

Full implementation plan at: ~/.claude/plans/okay-i-ll-try-to-moonlit-scroll.md
title: Build DB ↔ CSV bidirectional data sync (export_data + import_data commands)
